hero.title: Professor of Economic History
hero.description: Specialized in the study of public finances and the formation of the modern state in the 18th century.

about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: Academic Profile
about.p1: Since 1993 I have developed my teaching and research work in the Department of Historical Sciences of the University of Las Palmas de Gran Canaria. My academic background includes a degree and a doctorate in History from the University of Navarra, specializing in Economic History.
about.p2: I am currently part of the Research Group "Documentation, Heritage and Atlantic History" (DPHA) of the University Institute of Textual Analysis and Applications (IATEXT), of which I was coordinator between 2015 and 2025.
about.p3: Throughout my career I have assumed various academic and management responsibilities: Secretary of the Department of Historical Sciences, member of the University Senate, coordinator of the Economic History Area, coordinator of the GIR-DPHA and the Educational Innovation Group in Economy and History (GIZEH).

highlights.formation: Degree and PhD in History from the University of Navarra
highlights.teaching: Faculty of Economics, Business and Tourism - Faculty of Geography and History
highlights.research: Coordinator of the DPHA Group of the IATEXT Institute

research.title: Research
research.description: My work is oriented towards the History of Spain in the 18th century, especially the world of finance and the formation of the modern State.

projects.title: Research Projects
projects.description: Participation in competitive national and international research projects, focused on economic and fiscal history.

publications.title: Publications
publications.description: My academic production includes articles in scientific journals, books, and book chapters, as well as contributions to international congresses.

teaching.title: Teaching
teaching.experience: Uninterrupted university teaching activity since 1993, teaching economic history subjects at various academic levels.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: For academic inquiries, research collaborations, or additional information regarding my teaching activities, please feel free to reach out through the following official channels.

profile.title: PhD in History and Professor
profile.institute: University Institute of Textual Analysis and Applications (IATEXT)
profile.university: University of Las Palmas de Gran Canaria
profile.address: Department of Historical Sciences - IATEXT, Humanities Building, Obelisco Campus, 35003 Las Palmas de Gran Canaria
profile.bio: PhD in History and Professor of Economic History and Institutions. Specialized in the study of public finances and the formation of the modern state in the 18th century.
